lede: The full procure-to-pay flow in one screen: raise a purchase order, receive the goods into stock, then post the vendor bill with a built-in 3-way match that catches over-billing before A/P is booked. Everything a buyer needs — order, receipt, bill, payment — without leaving the list.

steps.title: Open Purchase Orders
steps.narration: From the Inventory sidebar, select Purchase Orders. This is the buyer's home base — every PO for the active branch, from draft to received to fully paid, in one filterable list.

steps.title: Filter by status and payment
steps.narration: Narrow the list fast: the Status filter (Draft / Approved / Received / Cancelled) and the Payment filter (Unpaid / Partial / Paid) stack, so 'Received + Unpaid' instantly surfaces exactly the orders that still owe a vendor bill.

steps.title: Raise a new purchase order
steps.narration: Click New Purchase Order. A side sheet opens to capture the supplier, terms, and the lines you're buying — no page reload, no separate screen.

steps.title: Pick the supplier
steps.narration: Choose the supplier the goods are coming from. The supplier drives the payable partner, the default credit terms, and — for designated buyers — whether VDS is withheld when the bill is paid.

steps.title: Set the payment terms
steps.narration: Cash purchases settle on receipt (no separate bill). Credit purchases accrue accounts payable — goods receipt books inventory against a GR/IR clearing account, and the vendor bill you post later clears it. Pick Credit for the standard bill-then-pay flow.

steps.title: Create the order
steps.narration: Add each line by barcode or SKU, then Create Purchase Order. It lands in Draft; approve it, and it's ready to receive when the goods arrive.

steps.title: Open a received PO
steps.narration: When goods arrive, receive the PO — stock posts to inventory and the GR/IR accrual is booked automatically. Then reopen it from the row's menu → View Details to bill it.

steps.title: Post the vendor bill
steps.narration: The detail sheet's action bar is pinned to the bottom, so Receive, Post Vendor Bill and Record Payment are always one click away — no scrolling past the line items. Click Post Vendor Bill to raise the payable.

steps.title: 3-way match: enter the vendor's actual invoice
steps.narration: This is the control that stops overpaying suppliers. The field pre-fills with the PO total; type the amount on the vendor's real paper invoice instead. The system reconciles billed vs. PO vs. received — within tolerance it posts silently; beyond tolerance it warns or blocks, and the variance is stamped on the journal entry for audit.

steps.title: Book the payable
steps.narration: Post Bill clears the GR/IR accrual and credits Accounts Payable (net of any withheld VDS), booking claimable input VAT in the same entry. The bill is now a matched, aging-tracked payable ready for Record Payment.
